sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion, sitting in 10th place with 52 points, host West Ham United, who are struggling in 17th position with 37 points, in Round 34 at the American Express Stadium in Falmer. This matchup promises to be intriguing given the disparity in league standings. Brighton will look to capitalize on their home advantage against a West Ham side desperate for points to climb away from the relegation zone. The referee, Darren England, averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Brighton have secured 2 wins, there have been 2 draws, and West Ham have managed 1 victory. Their most recent clash on December 21, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw, with Brighton's Carlos Baleba earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Historically, West Ham have gone 3 matches without a loss against Brighton in head-to-head encounters, though this streak is not currently active. Matches between these teams also consistently see less than 4.5 cards, having done so in all of their last 10 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Brighton are currently on a streak of 6 games without a win, though this run is not active, while West Ham are in the midst of a 6-game winless streak that continues into this match. Both teams are also struggling defensively, with Brighton failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 games and West Ham in their last 6. Brighton's matches have seen over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 7 games, and both teams have scored in 7 of Brighton's last 9 matches. Meanwhile, West Ham's games often feature less than 2.5 goals, occurring in 7 of their last 9 outings, though this trend is not currently active.

sections.subtitle: Brighton's Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Brighton boast a strong attacking presence with Danny Welbeck, who has scored 9 goals against an expected goals (xG) of 8.12, and Kaoru Mitoma, also with 9 goals from an xG of 8.76. Defensively, Joël Veltman stands out, being fouled 2.1 times per game, which ranks him 4th in the league for this metric. Brighton's season stats show they rank 8th for total goals scored with 57 and 5th for big chances created with 101. Their aerial prowess is evident with a 52.1% aerial duels won rate, ranking 4th in the league.

sections.subtitle: West Ham's Key Players and Stats
sections.text: West Ham rely heavily on Jarrod Bowen, who has scored 10 goals against an xG of 8.04 and provided 7 assists, earning a strong average Sofascore rating of 7.17. Mohammed Kudus is another standout, ranking 2nd in the league for successful dribbles per game with 3.0. Tomáš Souček has also contributed with 8 goals this season. Despite these individual performances, West Ham's season has been underwhelming, as reflected in their league position of 17th with only 37 points.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Brighton will feel the absence of key forward João Pedro, who has 10 goals and 6 assists this season, alongside midfielder Georginio Rutter with 5 goals. West Ham are also missing important players like Michail Antonio, a forward with notable physicality ranking 3rd in the league for long balls percentage at 11%. These absences could impact both teams' attacking fluidity, potentially leading to a more cautious game plan from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Standout Historical Performer
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Brighton, West Ham's goalkeeper Alphonse Aréola has been exceptional, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.96. With 6.6 saves per game across 5 starts, he has been a crucial barrier for West Ham, despite not contributing with goals or assists. His performances could be key if West Ham are to secure a positive result in this fixture at the American Express Stadium.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Game Expectations
sections.text: Brighton excel in possession, averaging 52.5% per game (7th in the league), and are effective in the final third with 91.9 successful passes per game (10th in the league). Their goal distribution shows a late surge, with 26.8% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. West Ham, conversely, also score frequently late, with 25.4% of their goals in the same period, but struggle with consistency. Given Brighton's rank of 3rd for accurate crosses with 172 and West Ham's defensive vulnerabilities, expect Brighton to exploit the flanks.
